parts.title: Transcript
parts.annotated_transcript_html:
Speaker 1: You now have some time to look at the questions on your screen. Now answer the questions on your screen. We're going to have some breakfast first in the dining area of the main building, and then we'll get started with the jobs.

Speaker 1: We'll be splitting into two groups, A and B. Most important today, and first on the list, is to clear the weeds from around the main walkway. As you can see, they have grown somewhat over the summer. So we need to clear those so visitors, and ourselves, can walk up and down the path easily. That's the first job for Group A, please.

Speaker 1: Group B will be moving over the bridge and working in the larger overgrown area. Yes, once again, we'll be cutting and clearing, though we have some special large lawnmowers and strimmers for this. So even though it is a huge area of land, it shouldn't take too long.

Speaker 1: Once we have this part finished, we'll be doing a little painting. As we make our way over there later, you'll probably notice the bridge looks a little neglected. We've got some special paint, which is the same as the paint on the main building. Group B will do this, while Group A prepares lunch.

Speaker 1: After lunch, we'll be going down to the greenhouses and vegetable patches. There are lots of vegetables to pick and bring in. That's job for Group A, so they'll be doing some of that and transferring them to these large green bags, as well as, yes, you've guessed it, some more weeding for Group B, putting that waste into the brown bags.

Speaker 1: Then, on to the farm shop. Group A will separate the different vegetables, wash them, and put them into the plastic storage boxes. The final job for Group B will be to collect some eggs from the chicken coops and take them to the shop before taking a turn serving customers at the till in the shop, while Group A makes us all a well-deserved cup of tea, before we close at six o'clock.
